candidate_hash_asserted_by_shared_evalua.evidence: All listed dev runs have metrics.json produced by myrec.eval.evaluator with the same candidate_manifest_sha256.

shared_evaluator_only.evidence: metrics.json and per_request_metrics.jsonl were generated by myrec.eval.evaluator for Random, B0a, B0b, B1, B2z, B7-bm25, and B7-bge.

history_sanity_b0b_vs_random.note: Batch 1 uses B0b as the history-only sanity check; B4 is deferred by doc 13.

diagnostics.summary: B1 BM25 instrument diagnostics passed, and the candidate pool is already strongly query-conditioned; original dominance gate remains failed.

b5_official_alignment.evidence: B5 is explicitly deferred out of Batch 1 in doc 13.

manual_top5_review.evidence: A 20-request top-5 review sheet was generated at reports/b1_bm25_top5_review.md, but it still requires human pass/fail review to satisfy doc 11 C2.

conclusion: C2 is not passed under the frozen gate because B1 BM25 is not significantly better than B0a Popularity, despite request-local BM25, CJK 2/3-gram, exact phrase boost, jieba, global item-catalog IDF, and character coverage variants. Follow-up diagnostics in reports/pps_c2_b1_diagnostics.json support the data-property explanation: BM25 responds to the true query, fixed candidates are already query-conditioned, and B0a matches train-only records. Per protocol, M3 remains exploratory until a human completes the top-5 review and explicitly revises the C2 rule or otherwise resolves the query-only baseline issue.
